svg.text: Integrative lab: ICMP/Traceroute · OSPF · BGP policy
traceroute: probe TTL k, read ICMP TIME EXCEEDED (type 11)

g.text: host ─TTL 1─→ R1 ─back─ ICMP TE → host learns R1
host ─TTL 2─→ R2 ─back─ ICMP TE → host learns R2
host ─TTL 3─→ R3 → ICMP ECHO REPLY (reached)
traceroute -I (ICMP probe) some firewalls drop
traceroute -U (UDP probe) default; some paths drop UDP
tcptraceroute -T -p 80 bypasses UDP/ICMP filters
Asterisks in column k ≠ lost data traffic — only lost probes.

svg.text: OSPF cost flip → blackhole, then recover

g.text: add cost 65535 on R1→R2 → SPF recomputes (≈30s)
During recompute: traffic to R2-hosts drops
Trace shows hop 2 missing during outage, then rerouted via R3
Recovery time ≈ HELLO dead (40s) + SPF delay (5s) + FIB
border R1: LOCAL_PREF 200 (route-map on inbound Cust-A)
border R2: LOCAL_PREF 150 (default)
→ all AS-egress picks R1 — even if R2 is physically closer
Prefix hijack: announce /24 with shorter AS_PATH than owner
Confirm via RADB/IRR — published route ≠ received route
1. traceroute before/after an OSPF cost flip
2. tcpdump on border showing hot-potato exit changing
3. AS_PATH of received /24 vs IRR/RADB published
4. one-page runbook "is it OSPF or is it BGP?"
